f0001.title: AI automates 5% of goods-and-services tasks by 2027
f0001.description: AI automates 5% of goods-and-services tasks by 2027. Resolves YES if, by December 31, 2027, market administrators judge that AI systems can perform, and are actually being deployed at scale to perform, at least 5% of goods-and-services economic tasks, weighted by their share of 2026 economic output — consistent with the Full Takeoff Model's frac_tasks_automated_goods definition (tasks where AI/compute-based production has displaced human labor as the dominant input). Because no single public statistic tracks this fraction directly, administrators will base their judgment on mainstream economic analyses, labor-automation and occupational studies, and industry deployment data available at resolution time, disclosing their reasoning. If the 5% threshold is not clearly reached, or the evidence is genuinely ambiguous, by the deadline, the market resolves NO. Prior from the FTM Monte Carlo (2000 trials, observed-2026-06-28 recalibration): 5.1%.

Resolves YES if, by December 31, 2044, market administrators judge that AI systems can perform, and are actually being deployed at scale to perform, at least 20% of goods-and-services economic tasks, weighted by their share of 2026 economic output — consistent with the Full Takeoff Model's frac_tasks_automated_goods definition (tasks where AI/compute-based production has displaced human labor as the dominant input). Because no single public statistic tracks this fraction directly, administrators will base their judgment on mainstream economic analyses, labor-automation and occupational studies, and industry deployment data available at resolution time, disclosing their reasoning. If the 20% threshold is not clearly reached, or the evidence is genuinely ambiguous, by the deadline, the market resolves NO. Prior from the FTM Monte Carlo (2000 trials, observed-2026-06-28 recalibration): 66.5%.

Resolves YES if, by December 31, 2030, market administrators judge that AI systems can perform, and are actually being deployed at scale to perform, at least 5% of R&D tasks — the research effort devoted to improving AI hardware and software — weighted by their share of 2026 R&D activity, consistent with the Full Takeoff Model's frac_tasks_automated_rnd definition. Because no single public statistic tracks this fraction directly, administrators will base their judgment on mainstream analyses of AI-driven research automation, lab and industry reporting, and academic studies available at resolution time, disclosing their reasoning. If the 5% threshold is not clearly reached, or the evidence is genuinely ambiguous, by the deadline, the market resolves NO. Prior from the FTM Monte Carlo (2000 trials, observed-2026-06-28 recalibration): 23.2%.

Resolves YES if, by December 31, 2031, market administrators judge that AI systems can perform, and are actually being deployed at scale to perform, at least 50% of R&D tasks — the research effort devoted to improving AI hardware and software — weighted by their share of 2026 R&D activity, consistent with the Full Takeoff Model's frac_tasks_automated_rnd definition. Because no single public statistic tracks this fraction directly, administrators will base their judgment on mainstream analyses of AI-driven research automation, lab and industry reporting, and academic studies available at resolution time, disclosing their reasoning. If the 50% threshold is not clearly reached, or the evidence is genuinely ambiguous, by the deadline, the market resolves NO. Prior from the FTM Monte Carlo (2000 trials, observed-2026-06-28 recalibration): 5.4%.

f0133.title: World economic growth exceeds 5%/year in 2027
f0133.description: World economic growth exceeds 5%/year in 2027. Resolves YES if the real global economic growth rate realized during calendar year 2027 exceeds 5%/year, as reported by the IMF World Economic Outlook (using its estimate for that year once published, even if labeled preliminary). This is a single-year measure: only growth realized within calendar year 2027 itself counts, not any other year and not a running maximum. If the IMF WEO series is discontinued or unavailable at resolution time, administrators will substitute the closest equivalent credible public estimate of global real GDP growth for 2027, such as the World Bank's Global Economic Prospects; resolution occurs once final or near-final annual figures are published, typically 6–18 months after year-end. Prior from the FTM Monte Carlo (2000 trials, observed-2026-06-28 recalibration): 6.7%.

f0201.title: World economic growth has exceeded 5%/year by 2029
f0201.description: World economic growth has exceeded 5%/year by 2029. Resolves YES if, for any calendar year from the market's creation through 2029 inclusive, the real global economic growth rate — as reported by the IMF World Economic Outlook (or, if unavailable, the World Bank's Global Economic Prospects) — has exceeded 5%/year in that single year. This tracks a running maximum: the market resolves YES as soon as any one qualifying year's confirmed growth clears the threshold, and remains YES even if growth subsequently falls back down, unlike the single-year gwp_growth markets. If the IMF WEO series is discontinued, administrators will substitute the closest equivalent credible public estimate of global real GDP growth for the relevant years. If no year through 2029 is confirmed to have cleared the threshold once final figures are published, the market resolves NO. Prior from the FTM Monte Carlo (2000 trials, observed-2026-06-28 recalibration): 12.9%.

Resolves YES if, for any calendar year from the market's creation through 2042 inclusive, the real global economic growth rate — as reported by the IMF World Economic Outlook (or, if unavailable, the World Bank's Global Economic Prospects) — has exceeded 5%/year in that single year. This tracks a running maximum: the market resolves YES as soon as any one qualifying year's confirmed growth clears the threshold, and remains YES even if growth subsequently falls back down, unlike the single-year gwp_growth markets. If the IMF WEO series is discontinued, administrators will substitute the closest equivalent credible public estimate of global real GDP growth for the relevant years. If no year through 2042 is confirmed to have cleared the threshold once final figures are published, the market resolves NO. Prior from the FTM Monte Carlo (2000 trials, observed-2026-06-28 recalibration): 69.3%.

Resolves YES if, for any calendar year from the market's creation through 2042 inclusive, the real global economic growth rate — as reported by the IMF World Economic Outlook (or, if unavailable, the World Bank's Global Economic Prospects) — has exceeded 20%/year in that single year. This tracks a running maximum: the market resolves YES as soon as any one qualifying year's confirmed growth clears the threshold, and remains YES even if growth subsequently falls back down, unlike the single-year gwp_growth markets. If the IMF WEO series is discontinued, administrators will substitute the closest equivalent credible public estimate of global real GDP growth for the relevant years. If no year through 2042 is confirmed to have cleared the threshold once final figures are published, the market resolves NO. Prior from the FTM Monte Carlo (2000 trials, observed-2026-06-28 recalibration): 46.9%.

f0268.title: Largest training run exceeds 1e28 FLOP by 2027
f0268.description: Largest training run exceeds 1e28 FLOP by 2027. Resolves YES if, on or before December 31, 2027, any single AI training run is confirmed to have used more than 1e28 FLOP of training compute, per Epoch AI's training-compute database (or, where Epoch has not yet published a figure, Epoch's methodology or a comparable independent estimate applied to public information about the run). Only completed or substantially completed training runs count; projected, announced-but-undisclosed, or rumored figures do not qualify until a credible compute estimate exists. If Epoch AI discontinues or stops maintaining this tracking, administrators will rely on the best available public estimate of the largest known training run's compute, using a comparable methodology, and will disclose which source they used. Prior from the FTM Monte Carlo (2000 trials, observed-2026-06-28 recalibration): 4.2%.

Resolves YES if, on or before December 31, 2043, any single AI training run is confirmed to have used more than 1e33 FLOP of training compute, per Epoch AI's training-compute database (or, where Epoch has not yet published a figure, Epoch's methodology or a comparable independent estimate applied to public information about the run). Only completed or substantially completed training runs count; projected, announced-but-undisclosed, or rumored figures do not qualify until a credible compute estimate exists. If Epoch AI discontinues or stops maintaining this tracking, administrators will rely on the best available public estimate of the largest known training run's compute, using a comparable methodology, and will disclose which source they used. Prior from the FTM Monte Carlo (2000 trials, observed-2026-06-28 recalibration): 54.9%.

f0321.title: Compute investment exceeds 0.5% of world output by 2027
f0321.description: Compute investment exceeds 0.5% of world output by 2027. Resolves YES if, on or before December 31, 2027, credible industry or academic analyses (e.g., Epoch AI's compute/hardware spending estimates, IMF or World Bank data, or comparable analyst estimates of global AI-relevant capital expenditure) indicate that annual worldwide investment in AI-relevant compute — hardware production and deployment for AI — has reached or exceeded 0.5% of that year's gross world product. Because no single authoritative real-time tracker exists for this ratio, administrators will weigh the best available public estimates at resolution time and disclose their reasoning if sources diverge. If no year through 2027 is confirmed to have cleared the threshold, the market resolves NO. Prior from the FTM Monte Carlo (2000 trials, observed-2026-06-28 recalibration): 11.4%.

f0388.title: Hardware price-performance improves 10x over 2026 by 2031
f0388.description: Hardware price-performance improves 10x over 2026 by 2031. Resolves YES if, on or before December 31, 2031, Epoch AI's hardware price-performance dataset (effective FLOP/s per inflation-adjusted dollar for state-of-the-art AI chips) shows an improvement of at least 10x relative to the best available value for 2026. This tracks hardware cost-efficiency only, not algorithmic/software efficiency gains (see the separate sw_ratio markets for that). If Epoch AI discontinues this dataset, administrators will substitute an equivalent credible public estimate of AI-chip price-performance trends, applying the same 10x comparison against the 2026 baseline and disclosing the substitute source. Prior from the FTM Monte Carlo (2000 trials, observed-2026-06-28 recalibration): 3.5%.

Resolves YES if, on or before December 31, 2036, Epoch AI's hardware price-performance dataset (effective FLOP/s per inflation-adjusted dollar for state-of-the-art AI chips) shows an improvement of at least 10x relative to the best available value for 2026. This tracks hardware cost-efficiency only, not algorithmic/software efficiency gains (see the separate sw_ratio markets for that). If Epoch AI discontinues this dataset, administrators will substitute an equivalent credible public estimate of AI-chip price-performance trends, applying the same 10x comparison against the 2026 baseline and disclosing the substitute source. Prior from the FTM Monte Carlo (2000 trials, observed-2026-06-28 recalibration): 47.6%.

f0429.title: AI software efficiency improves 10x over 2026 by 2029
f0429.description: AI software efficiency improves 10x over 2026 by 2029. Resolves YES if, on or before December 31, 2029, credible algorithmic-efficiency research (e.g., Epoch AI's algorithmic progress estimates, or comparable independent studies measuring how the compute required to reach a fixed AI capability level changes over time) indicates that the training or inference compute needed to achieve a benchmark level of AI capability has fallen by a factor of at least 10x relative to 2026 methods. This tracks algorithmic/software efficiency only, not hardware price-performance gains (see the separate hw_ratio markets for that). If Epoch AI's estimates are unavailable or discontinued, administrators will use the best available equivalent public research, applying the same 10x comparison against the 2026 baseline and disclosing the substitute source. Prior from the FTM Monte Carlo (2000 trials, observed-2026-06-28 recalibration): 4.8%.

This tracks algorithmic/software efficiency only, not hardware price-performance gains (see the separate hw_ratio markets for that). This tracks algorithmic/software efficiency only, not hardware price-performance gains (see the separate hw_ratio markets for that). If Epoch AI's estimates are unavailable or discontinued, administrators will use the best available equivalent public research, applying the same 10,000x comparison against the 2026 baseline and disclosing the substitute source. Prior from the FTM Monte Carlo (2000 trials, observed-2026-06-28 recalibration): 37.7%.

f0471.title: Full automation of goods-and-services work by 2032
f0471.description: Full automation of goods-and-services work by 2032. Resolves YES if, on or before December 31, 2032, market administrators judge that essentially all goods-and-services economic tasks (100% of tasks weighted by 2026 economic value) can be performed, and are being performed at scale, by AI systems without human labor input — consistent with the Full Takeoff Model's full-automation threshold (frac_tasks_automated_goods reaching 100%). Because no clean public statistic tracks full automation directly, administrators will base this judgment on mainstream economic analyses, labor-market data, and credible reporting on the scope of any remaining human-performed work, available at resolution time. If the evidence is ambiguous or full automation is not clearly reached by the deadline, the market resolves NO. Prior from the FTM Monte Carlo (2000 trials, observed-2026-06-28 recalibration): 3.8%.

f0485.description: Full automation of R&D work by 2032. Because no clean public statistic tracks full R&D automation directly, administrators will base this judgment on mainstream analyses of AI-driven research automation, lab reporting, and academic studies available at resolution time. f0499.title: AGI training requirements are met by 2032
f0499.description: AGI training requirements are met by 2032. Resolves YES if, on or before December 31, 2032, market administrators judge that the largest completed AI training run (per Epoch AI's training-compute database or an equivalent credible estimate) has reached or exceeded the effective training compute that credible contemporaneous AI-forecasting research (e.g., Epoch AI or comparable compute-requirements analyses) estimates would be needed to train a model capable of fully automating goods-and-services tasks, consistent with the Full Takeoff Model's agi_year definition. This is a training-compute-sufficiency threshold, distinct from whether that capability has actually been built or deployed at scale (see the full_auto markets for deployment). Because the compute-requirement estimate itself is uncertain and may be revised as research progresses, administrators will use their judgment of the best available public estimates at resolution time rather than any single fixed number, and will disclose which estimates they relied on. Prior from the FTM Monte Carlo (2000 trials, observed-2026-06-28 recalibration): 3.7%.
